title: Moraea brachygyne
taxonomy: Moraea brachygyne is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae, commonly known as the iris family. This genus is further divided into several sections based on their characteristics and growth habits. Moraea brachygyne is typically classified as a member of the subgenus Microanthus. Within this subgenus, it is often grouped with other species that share similar characteristics, such as flower shape and size. The exact classification of Moraea brachygyne may vary depending on the source and the most recent taxonomic updates.

intro: Moraea brachygyne, also known as the short-stemmed moraea, is a plant species within the family Iridaceae, characterized by its striking blue-purple flowers and upright habit. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with its health benefits attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, digestive issues, and skin problems. The bioactive constituents of Moraea brachygyne include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Moraea brachygyne is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the production of herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures.
